title: Is dollar cost averaging better than lump sum?
question_body: Community question about is dollar cost averaging better than lump sum?
answer_body: Historically lump sum has a higher expected return, but averaging reduces timing risk and regret.
combined_text: Is dollar cost averaging better than lump sum? Community question about is dollar cost averaging better than lump sum? Historically lump sum has a higher expected return, but averaging reduces timing risk and regret. investing dollar-cost-averaging risk

title: Is dollar cost averaging better than lump sum?
question_body: Community question about is dollar cost averaging better than lump sum?
answer_body: If the money is already in cash, averaging it in over a few months is a reasonable behavioural compromise.

title: How are capital gains taxed?
question_body: Community question about how are capital gains taxed?
answer_body: It depends on holding period, income, jurisdiction and cost basis. Separate short-term from long-term and keep records.
combined_text: How are capital gains taxed? Community question about how are capital gains taxed? It depends on holding period, income, jurisdiction and cost basis. Separate short-term from long-term and keep records. tax capital-gains investing

title: What records do I need for investment taxes?
question_body: Community question about what records do i need for investment taxes?
answer_body: Keep trade confirmations, cost basis, dividend statements and broker 1099s; reconstruct basis before you sell.

title: Do I need a financial advisor?
question_body: Community question about do i need a financial advisor?
answer_body: A fee-only fiduciary helps with complex tax, estate or retirement planning; simple investing may only need a low-cost index fund.
combined_text: Do I need a financial advisor? Community question about do i need a financial advisor? A fee-only fiduciary helps with complex tax, estate or retirement planning; simple investing may only need a low-cost index fund. advisor investing retirement fees

title: How do I start investing with little money?
question_body: Community question about how do i start investing with little money?
answer_body: Many brokers allow fractional shares, so you can begin with very small amounts.

title: Should I invest in ETFs or individual stocks?
question_body: Community question about should i invest in etfs or individual stocks?
answer_body: For most investors, diversified low-cost ETFs cut idiosyncratic risk and need less monitoring than single stocks.

title: Should I pay extra on my mortgage or invest?
question_body: Community question about should i pay extra on my mortgage or invest?
answer_body: Paying down a low fixed-rate mortgage is a guaranteed but modest return; investing has higher expected but uncertain returns.
combined_text: Should I pay extra on my mortgage or invest? Community question about should i pay extra on my mortgage or invest? Paying down a low fixed-rate mortgage is a guaranteed but modest return; investing has higher expected but uncertain returns. mortgage investing debt

title: What fees matter when choosing an ETF?
question_body: Community question about what fees matter when choosing an etf?
answer_body: A 0.5% vs 0.05% expense ratio compounds into a large gap over decades.

title: Should I refinance my mortgage?
question_body: Community question about should i refinance my mortgage?
answer_body: Watch the term reset: lowering the rate but restarting 30 years can raise total interest paid.
